steps.title: Identify the slope from line k using the parallel lines property
steps.content:
Line $k$ is given as $y = 3x - 2$ in slope-intercept form. The slope of line $k$ is 3. Since line $j$ is parallel to line $k$, and parallel lines have the same slope, line $j$ must also have slope 3.

Therefore, the equation of line $j$ has the form $y = 3x + b$, where $b$ is the unknown y-intercept.

steps.title: Use the point to find the y-intercept
steps.content: Line $j$ passes through the point $(0, 1)$. Substitute this point into $y = 3x + b$: $$1 = 3(0) + b$$ $$1 = b$$ Therefore, the equation of line $j$ is $y = 3x + 1$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
